description_official: DRAFT — verify against current Space Force enlisted classification. Space Force cyberspace operators execute defensive and offensive cyber missions in support of space operations.
description_plain: Cyber for the Space Force. Closer to Air Force 1D7X1 than any pure space operations role — Space Force inherited its cyber structure from the Air Force and is still evolving its own standards. Expect to work alongside Air Force cyber counterparts early in your career.
description_tldr: Space Force cyberspace operator — cyber defense and offensive operations in support of space missions.

asvab.raw_requirement: G (General): 64 (DRAFT — verify; Space Force currently uses Air Force aptitude composites)
asvab.notes: Space Force uses Air Force aptitude composites pending its own system.

training_pipeline.description: Space Force currently uses Air Force BMT.
DRAFT — verify current Space Force pipeline.

salary_range_usd.notes: Space/cyber cleared roles pay at the top of range.

branch_specific.delta: Assigned to Space Operations Command (SpOC) cyber units

daily_life: SCIF-based cyber watch and response, similar to Air Force cyber but scoped to Space Force mission systems and satellite networks. Day shifts and mid/night rotating watches are common. Strong collaboration with NSA, USCYBERCOM, and Space Operations Command.

faq.question: Is Space Force really separate from the Air Force?
faq.answer: Yes — Space Force is an independent service under the Department of the Air Force. It has its own ranks (Specialist 1-4, Sergeant, Technical Sergeant, Master Sergeant, Chief Master Sergeant) and classification system. Many Space Force members transferred from the Air Force in 2020-2021.

faq.question: Do Space Force members go to space?
faq.answer: No — Space Force operates space-based systems from Earth. Astronauts belong to NASA. Space Force handles satellites, ground control, and cyber defense for space missions.

notable_holders.name: Chief Master Sergeant of the Space Force Roger A. Towberman
notable_holders.notable_for: First senior enlisted advisor of the Space Force.
notable_holders.notes: Not 5C0X1-specific; representative Space Force senior enlisted leadership.

promotion_speed.fast_promote_notes: Space Force is small and still building out its senior enlisted ranks — early promotion has been comparatively fast for strong performers in cyber and space operations.
promotion_speed.slow_promote_notes: The force is small enough that specific billets can create bottlenecks.
promotion_speed.cutting_score_note: Space Force uses a modified version of WAPS currently; its own evaluation system is being finalized.

sources.name: Space Force enlisted classification (DRAFT — Space Force is still publishing formal directives)
